meta_description: Learn how to Claude Code skills setup and overcome no portfolio. A Advanced-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Claude Code Setup to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Overcome the 'no portfolio' trap by mastering Claude Code. An advanced guide for students to build high-impact AI projects using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: Overcoming the 'no portfolio' barrier requires shipping production-grade AI tools, not just tutorials. By integrating Claude Code with Antigravity, you gain an autonomous development engine that accelerates code velocity. This setup transforms your workflow, allowing you to build, debug, and deploy sophisticated SaaS features that demonstrate real-world engineering competency to employers.
content.intro:
You stare at the blank screen, the cursor blinking in a rhythmic taunt. You have the theory, the desire, and the ambition, but your GitHub profile remains a graveyard of abandoned tutorials and 'hello world' scripts. The industry doesn't care about your certificates; it cares about the complexity of the systems you have architected and deployed.

This is where the paradigm shifts. We are moving beyond manual coding into the era of AI-assisted engineering. By mastering Claude Code in tandem with Antigravity, you stop being a student who writes code and start being an architect who orchestrates it. This guide is your blueprint to building the high-fidelity projects that turn 'no experience' into 'hired'.

steps.title: Environment Orchestration with Antigravity
steps.description: Initialize your workspace to handle complex dependencies and AI-agent interactions without manual boilerplate fatigue.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a professional SaaS boilerplate with TypeScript, Tailwind, and Supabase integration, ensuring modular architecture for future AI agent hooks.

steps.title: Configuring Claude Code Agentic Context
steps.description: Provide the agent with deep context of your project architecture so it understands your design patterns before writing a single line.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my project structure and create a comprehensive .claudeprompt file that defines my coding style, preferred library versions, and architectural constraints.

steps.title: Implementing Core Business Logic
steps.description: Leverage Claude Code to build out complex features while you focus on high-level system design and security.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a secure, edge-optimized API route for user authentication that validates JWTs and handles rate limiting with Redis.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "Implement secure auth middleware with Redis rate limiting"

steps.title: Automated Testing & Edge-Case Debugging
steps.description: Use AI to write unit tests for your logic, ensuring your portfolio projects are production-ready and bug-free.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Vitest suite covering all edge cases for the current auth implementation, including invalid tokens and expired sessions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "Generate and run unit tests for the auth module"

steps.title: Deployment & Documentation
steps.description: Finalize your project with professional README files and CI/CD pipelines that impress recruiters.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a professional README.md that highlights the technical challenges solved, the tech stack, and a clear guide on how to deploy this project.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "Generate a professional README.md and a GitHub Actions deployment workflow"

pro_tips: Always define a custom .claudeprompt file to prevent the agent from using outdated libraries or discouraged legacy patterns.
Treat your Claude Code history as a 'development journal'—commit your chat logs to a private repo to track your engineering thought process.
Use the 'Review' mode in Claude Code to force the agent to explain the 'why' behind its code before you accept a change.
When building for your portfolio, use Antigravity to inject complex documentation into your code comments so the project is maintainable for future team members.
Always run your Claude-generated code through a static analysis tool like ESLint or Biome before committing to ensure the AI hasn't introduced linting debt.
resources: GitHub Best Practices for Portfolios: https://github.com/features/actions
content.next_steps: Once you have completed your first feature, deploy it to Vercel, write a LinkedIn post explaining the architectural challenge you solved, and tag the AI Builder community to get peer feedback.
content.conversion_block: You now have the tools to build faster, cleaner, and more complex projects than 99% of your peers. Stop waiting for permission to build and start shipping code that proves your worth. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
